title: Fernandezia cuencae
taxonomy: Fernandezia cuencae is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It belongs to the subfamily Orchidoideae and tribe Vanilleae. Fernandezia cuencae is further classified into the genus Fernandezia, which is a small group of orchids native to South America. Within this genus, it is a distinct species characterized by its unique characteristics and features. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary history and relationships with other orchid species.

intro: Fernandezia cuencae, commonly known as the "Queñua de la Cordillera," is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the Andean region of South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Fernandezia cuencae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory infections, fever, and other health conditions.
